title: rubus × pulverulentus var. fallacinus
intro: Rubus × pulverulentus var. fallacinus, also known as the false black raspberry, is a hybrid plant of the Rubus genus, characterized by its black raspberry-like appearance but lack of edible fruit. The false black raspberry has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of the false black raspberry typically involve drying the leaves and using them in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
